title: verilog-ethernet ARP retry counter integer overflow
tagline: An 8-bit counter vs a 16-bit limit — ARP retries forever.
hook_30s: This ARP block retries a lookup until a 16-bit retry limit, then gives up. But the counter is only 8 bits. Set a limit above 255 and the counter wraps back to zero before it ever reaches the limit — so it never gives up, and the request retries forever. ChipGPT proves the overflow formally and in simulation.

proof.note: Arithmetic width bug — formal finds a reachable limit where give_up never asserts; a directed test drives 300 retries and watches give_up stay low.

steps.agent: Task: check the ARP retry counter against its design requirement (give up at retry_limit).
Reading the requirement: the retry counter must be able to represent every value up to retry_limit, or it wraps and never gives up.
Parsing arp.sv. Comparing the counter width against the limit field width.
retry_q is 8 bits; retry_limit_i is 16 bits; they are compared directly.

OVERFLOW. retry_q is logic [7:0] (line 13) but is compared against the 16-bit retry_limit_i (line 22). For any limit > 255, retry_q wraps 255->0 before reaching it, so give_up_o never asserts and the ARP request retries forever. CWE-190.
Generating the overflow assertion and checking it formally.

artifact.explanation: If the configured limit exceeds the counter's range, give_up can never be reached — a reachable failure.
artifact.sva:
assert property (@(posedge clk_i)
  (retry_limit_i <= 8'hFF));  // counter can only ever represent <= 255
artifact.result: SymbiYosys (z3): counterexample at retry_limit_i = 256 — give_up_o unreachable

steps.agent: Directed test: retry_limit_i = 300, pulse req_sent_i 400 times, watch give_up_o.

artifact.name: t_arp_retry_overflow
artifact.result_on_buggy: FAIL — give_up_o stays 0; retry_q wraps 255->0 (loops forever)

artifact.title: ARP retry counter integer overflow
artifact.root_cause: 8-bit retry_q compared against a 16-bit retry_limit_i; counter wraps before reaching limits > 255.
artifact.fix: Widen retry_q to 16 bits (match the limit field).
artifact.impact: For large retry limits the ARP lookup never abandons — the request retries forever.
